summary: Surface body headings in the extractor and decide require_sections, retiring its Indeterminate stub so a declared section clause stops silently no-op'ing.

edit.description: Add a deterministically-extracted `headings: Vec<String>` to `Features` (ATX `#`..`######` heading text from the byte-faithful body; a `#` inside a fenced code block is not a heading). Populate it in both `skill_features` and `rule_features`. Update the module-level doc list of decidable features and the unit tests (assert headings exposed for a skill and a rule).

Replace the `RequireSections` `Outcome::Indeterminate` arm with a real decision over `features.headings` (one finding per missing named section). Drop `require_sections` from the honest-bound doc paragraph (now only `dependency-exists` is Indeterminate). Add `headings` to the test `features()` helper, split `indeterminate_predicates_neither_pass_nor_fire` to cover only `dependency-exists`, and add a require_sections fires/clean test.

tests.asserts: skill_features and rule_features expose the body's ATX headings deterministically; a no-heading body yields an empty list; a `#` inside a fenced code block is not counted.
a require_sections clause fires (one finding per absent heading) when a named section is missing and is silent when every named heading is present; dependency-exists stays Indeterminate.

acceptance: A require_sections clause now produces a true-positive finding over a body missing a named heading instead of silently neither passing nor failing; `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --all --check` are green.
notes: Closes the last fork-free decidable gap in the in-crate algebra (10-contracts.md, structural `require_sections`). Touches no contract template â€” the skill/rule contracts carry no require_sections clause, so the acceptance snapshots in tests/acceptance.rs are unaffected; this only makes the primitive genuinely decidable when an author uses it. dependency-exists stays deferred â€” it needs the declared dependency model, gated on the (model-declaration-format) fork.
